# Curator Receipt

## Decision

Kept: this source should enter the institute as a serious research artifact candidate.

## Why

The source is an unpublished research manuscript with clear empirical substance: it studies 129,134 GitHub projects, reports estimated adoption rates, and presents measured commit-level and project-level findings about coding-agent use in the wild. This is institute-worthy under the criteria for serious preprints and real experimental or field-result reporting.

## Limits

# Curator Receipt

## Decision

Kept: this source should enter the institute as a serious research artifact candidate.

## Why

The source is an unpublished research manuscript with clear empirical substance: it studies 129,134 GitHub projects, reports estimated adoption rates, and presents measured commit-level and project-level findings about coding-agent use in the wild. This is institute-worthy under the criteria for serious preprints and real experimental or field-result reporting.

## Limits

This is a preprint rather than a formal published paper, and its findings rely on trace-based heuristics for detecting agent use, so heuristic detection limits should remain visible downstream.

# Curator Receipt

## Decision

Kept: this source should enter the institute as a serious research artifact candidate.

## Why

The source is an unpublished research manuscript with clear empirical substance: it studies 129,134 GitHub projects, reports estimated adoption rates, and presents measured commit-level and project-level findings about coding-agent use in the wild. This is institute-worthy under the criteria for serious preprints and real experimental or field-result reporting.

## Limits

This is a preprint rather than a formal published paper, and its findings rely on trace-based heuristics for detecting agent use, so heuristic detection limits should remain visible downstream.
